scope_note: QA Orbit Satellite Ramification Cert. Primary source: Wall, D.D. (1960). Fibonacci Series Modulo m. American Mathematical Monthly, 67(6), 525-532 (matrix representation of the Fibonacci recursion mod m and its periodicity, the Pisano period pi(5)=20 used here). Ireland, K. & Rosen, M. (1990). A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory, 2nd ed., Springer, ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 (quadratic residue criterion for irreducibility of a quadratic over F_p, used for the mod-3 case; repeated-root/ramification behavior at the discriminant prime, used for the mod-5 case). Certifies why orbit_family's period-8 (satellite) classification exists and why the fast divisor shortcut in qa_orbit_rules.py under-counts by exactly 32 satellites whenever 5 divides the modulus. Proof: qa_step(b,e,m) is conjugate to the Fibonacci matrix M=[[0,1],[1,1]] on (Z/mZ)^2; x^2-x-1 has discriminant 5, which is irreducible mod 3 (giving every nonzero mod-3 vector exact period 8) and has a repeated root mod 5 (giving a 4-element eigenspace of period 4, via the Jordan-block structure at the ramified prime). CRT/lcm composition of these two facts produces exactly 8x4=32 satellites invisible to the divisor shortcut whenever 5 | m. This is diagnostic of the existing canonical orbit_family/orbit_period functions, not a replacement for them.
